revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Gosashi tomb]][[fr:Tombe de Gosashi]][[ja:五社神古墳]][[zh:五社神古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Gosashi Kofun |image = File:Gosashi Kofun, haisho.jpg |part_of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) |location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|qid=Q85879846|1=Misasagi-chō}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.706225|N|135.785339|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |map_size = Mound length: 267 [[:en:Meter|m]]
Height: 27 m (Round rear mound) |burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Tateana-style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Contains {{ill|Nagamochigata Sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata Sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Accompanying Mounds = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site1) 5 |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th Century|1=4th century}} |Interred Person = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site) {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Saki Tatenami Ike no E no Misasagi" |Notable Points = 12th largest map_size in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun sizes (Japan Nationwide Edition)] (Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018).
Early example of {{ill|Zōde|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} |map = Nara city |type = Zenpokoen-fun - South-Southeast |map_dot_label = Gosashi Kofun |map_dot_label = Bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} }} [[File:Gosashi Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} Saki Tatenami Ike no E no Misasagi Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] {{panoViewer|Gosashi Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree spherical image of Saki Tatenami Ike no E no Misasagi Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Gosashi Kofun''' (五社神古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, Yamamuro Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred person is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, under the title "Saki Tatenami Ike no E no Misasagi." It is the 12th largest Kofun in Japan, presumed to have been constructed in the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th Century|1=4th century}} (early middleperiod of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}{{small|Upper left: '''Gosashi Kofun''' (Designated as Empress Jingū's tomb)
Lower middle: {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Designated as {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}'s tomb)
Lower right: {{ill|Saki Ryōyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Designated as Princess {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s tomb)
Bottom: {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Designated as {{ill|Emperor Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q232955}}'s tomb)
{{National Aerial Photography}}.}}]] Located in the northern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}} on Saki Hill, the massive {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} was constructed by cutting off a hill ridge. The name "Gosashi" derives from a shrine once present at the top of the rear mound. Historical records reveal an instance of grave robbery in 1849 (Kaei 2). Currently, it is managed as a Historic Site1 tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, though archaeological investigations of the mound's base were conducted in 2003 (Heisei 15), and scholarly access to the site was first permitted in 2008. The mound's type is asymmetrical, with the front mound oriented southward. The rear mound consists of four tiers, while the front mound consists of three tiers. The estimated restored length of the mound is 267 meters, making it the largest in the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the 12th largest in Japan. The western constricted area is believed to have an {{ill|Zōde|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, and surface findings include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and various types of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. While surrounded by a moat today, it is speculated to have originally lacked one. The burial facility is presumed to contain a {{ill|Tateana-style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a {{ill|Nagamochigata Sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata Sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, as indicated by Edo-period records of grave robbery. == Archaeological History == * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|qid=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} 2 ({{ill|1849|en|1849|qid=Q7650|lt=1849|1=1849}}) – Tomb was looted (historical records identify it as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|qid=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}}).{{Sfn|Fumiaki Imao|2014|pp=39-47}} * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3-4 ({{ill|1863|en|1863|qid=Q7701|lt=1863|1=1863}}-{{ill|1864|en|1864|qid=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}}) – Renovations during the Bunkyū period and official Historic Site as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (previously believed to be primarily the mausoleum of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|lt=Hibasu-hime|lt=Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|lt=Princess Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}).{{Sfn|Nara City History – Archaeology Volume|1971|pp=126-129}}{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period – The {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}} (now the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) confirmed its Historic Site as the mausoleum of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|1889|en|1889|qid=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} (Meiji 22), October – New stone fence and iron gate installed.{{Sfn|Nara City History – Archaeology Volume|1971|pp=126-129}} * {{ill|2003|en|2003|qid=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}} fiscal year ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 15) – Preliminary survey and external type survey conducted as part of maintenance work on the Kofun (carried out by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|qid=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}} of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}).{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} * {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20), February 22 – First academic investigation of the imperial mausoleum conducted by representatives from 16 archaeological and historical societies.[http://www.asahi.com/culture/news_culture/OSK200802220100.html "First academic inspection of an imperial tomb – Empress Jingū's Mausoleum in Nara"] (Asahi Shimbun, February 23, 2008). == Kofun == [[File:CG Gosashi Kofun 1 7p.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{center|3D CG rendering of {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|lt=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}}}]] The estimated restored area of the Kofun are as follows, based on the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} survey conducted in the 2003 fiscal year ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 15).{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} * '''Total mound length:''' Approximately 267 meters * '''Rear circular section''' – Constructed in four tiers. '''* Diameter: ''' Approximately 190 meters (east-west) '''* Height: ''' Approximately 27 meters '''* Mound top diameter: ''' Approximately 40 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' – Constructed in three tiers. '''* Width: ''' Approximately 150 meters '''* Length: ''' Approximately 115 meters '''* Height: ''' Approximately 20.5 meters * '''Constricted section''' '''* Width: ''' Approximately 110 meters * '''Protruding terrace''' – Estimated to exist on the western side of the constricted section. '''* Estimated area: ''' Approximately 28 meters (length) × 83 meters (width) The first tier of the mound terminates at the rear of the circular section and does not completely encircle the structure.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} Previously, the total mound length was thought to be 275 meters based on survey maps,{{Sfn|Nara City History – Archaeology Volume|1971|pp=126-129}}{{Sfn|Sashiro Tateretsu Ikeue Mausoleum (Heibonsha)|1981}} but the above values were revised based on an Imperial Household Agency survey at the base of the mound.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}}{{Sfn|Fumiaki Imao|2014|pp=39-47}} Although a surrounding moat currently encircles the Kofun, several historical documents do not mention such a moat. Based on its current type, it is believed that the mound originally did not have one.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} Some theories suggest that this moat was created as part of later restoration work during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} period.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} While the existence of the moat remains uncertain, there is evidence suggesting that a peripheral terrace, believed to be part of the original construction, exists around the Kofun.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} Additionally, a natural hill remains on the southern side of the front rectangular section. Some theories propose that, before the hill was cut, it originally typeed a continuous ridge with Gosashi Kofun.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} However, the Imperial Household Agency's survey found a significant gap between the mound and the hill, leading to a skeptical view regarding this theory.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} File:CG Gosashi Kofun 2 7p.jpg|3D CG rendering (view from the south) File:CG Gosashi Kofun 3 7p.jpg|3D CG rendering (frontal view of the front rectangular section) File:CG Gosashi Kofun 4 7p.jpg|3D CG rendering (view from the northwest) File:CG Gosashi Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3D CG rendering (view from the north) File:CG Gosashi Kofun 6 7p.jpg|3D CG rendering (side view) == Interred_individual ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Mausoleum names of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} ! ! {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}
(13th Emperor) ! {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
(14th Emperor Chūai's Empress) |- ! | [[File:Emperor Seimu.jpg|100px]] | [[File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|70px]] |- ! {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} | Sahiro Tateretsu Mausoleum | Sahiro Tateretsu Mausoleum |- ! {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} | Tateretsu South Mountain Mausoleum | Tateretsu North Mountain Mausoleum |- ! {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} | Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-nochi Mausoleum | Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum |- ! Present-day | Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-nochi Mausoleum
({{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}) | Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum
({{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |}
The actual buried figure in {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remains unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, the consort of the 14th Emperor, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}.{{Sfn|Imperial Household Agency|1934|p.9}}{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}}"Compilation of Mausoleum Topographic maps (Abridged Edition)," Imperial Household Agency Shoryōbu Mausoleum Section, Gakuseisha, 2014, pp. 402-403. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}},{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}} Empress Jingū's mausoleum is recorded as "Sahiro Tateretsu Mausoleum."{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}} In the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}},{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}} a later examination determined that the mausoleums of Emperor Seimu and Empress Jingū had been misidentified. Consequently, the northern mausoleum (Tateretsu North Mountain Mausoleum) was reassigned to Empress Jingū, while the southern mausoleum (Tateretsu South Mountain Mausoleum) was assigned to Emperor Seimu.{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}} The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'s {{ill|Shoryoryō|ja|諸陵寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoryō|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632153}} records state that Empress Jingū's mausoleum, categorized as a distant tomb, was named "Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum," covering a territory of 2 chō (approximately 218 meters) east-west and 2 chō north-south, with five households assigned annually for maintenance.{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}} Additionally, during the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} era in the 12th year (870), amid the {{ill|Jōgan Invasions of Silla|ja|新羅の入寇#貞観の入寇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan Invasions of Silla|1=Sillagu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8012917}}, and again in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|qid=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268) during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|qid=Q208156|lt=Mongol Invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}, imperial envoys were dispatched to the mausoleum.{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}} Later, records regarding Empress Jingū's burial site were lost once again. By {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 10 (1697), during the {{ill|Genroku Mausoleum Survey|ja|元禄探陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Mausoleum Survey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (presently Historic Site1 as the mausoleum of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|lt=Hibasu-hime|lt=Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|lt=Princess Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) was identified as her tomb, while {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|qid=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}}. However, in {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3 (1863), during the {{ill|Bunkyū Mausoleum Restoration|ja|文久の修陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū Mausoleum Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, based on historical documents such as the "Yamato Province Soekami District Kyōhoku Ichijō Banda map" (京北班田図) and "Saidai-ji Site Ancient map," Gosashi Kofun was reassigned as Empress Jingū’s mausoleum, a Historic Site that continues to this day.{{Sfn|Sahiro Tateretsu Ike-no-ue Mausoleum (National History)}}{{Sfn|Fumiaki Imao|2014|pp=39-47}} Additionally, due to the reassignment during the Bunkyū mausoleum restoration, eight stone lanterns that were originally donated between {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|qid=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)}} 2 and {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 2 (1745–1790) for the then-Historic Site1 Empress Jingū Mausoleum (Saki Misasagiyama Kofun) were later relocated to the worship area of Gosashi Kofun, where they remain today.{{Sfn|Shoryōbu Bulletin No. 56|2005|pp=5-54}} ==See Also== Gosashi Kofun == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五社神古墳|version=99994898|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:五社神古墳]] [[ja:遺跡歴]] [[ja:墳丘]] [[ja:Interred Persons]] [[fr:Tombe de Gosashi]] [[zh:%E4%BA%94%E7%A4%BE%E7%A5%9E%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Gosashi Kofun}} [[en:Gosashi tomb]] [[zh:五社神古墳]] {{Commons category|Gosashi Kofun}} [[en:Gosashi tomb]] [[zh:五社神古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q5587136}} [[Category:Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
